tasks_completed.changes: BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 3 → 5 in gate.py:54; docstring lines 6-7 updated 'capped at 3' → 'capped at 5'; CLAUDE.md line 46 updated 'Bash capped at 3 consecutive same-name calls' → 'Bash capped at 5 consecutive same-name calls'. scripts/execute-push.py: working tree already had stale text removed (grep returns zero results — AC1 satisfied).
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE=3 was too restrictive for parallel-worker pipelines that needed 4-5 consecutive Bash calls. Raised to 5 to allow the extra calls without blocking.

tasks_completed.description: Create scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py — canonical aggregate writer for parallel-dev cycles
tasks_completed.changes: New 432-line Python script. Mirrors PER_WORKER_ROLE_FIRST_RE, PER_WORKER_TASK_FIRST_RE, CANONICAL_RE, NON_WORKER_LABELS, NON_WORKER_LABEL_RE from pretool-aggregate-check.py exactly. --task-id (required) + --dry-run (optional). Derives docs/dev/ from CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env var or Path(__file__).parent.parent. Validates shards: non-empty task_id/request_id, non-empty baseline_head_sha, explicit baseline_dirty_snapshot key, all dev.status==completed. Canonical path uses full task_id (not bare timestamp). Canonical validation loads and compares parallel_workers + baseline_head_sha. Fail-closed on validation error. stdout JSON: {status:ok, action:aggregated|validated|skipped, output_path:..., reason:...}. Exit 0/1/2.
tasks_completed.rationale: Replaces the forbidden inline-Python approach for aggregate construction. Named script enables close.md Step 0 and QA verification.

tasks_completed.changes: Removed 'do NOT add a separate script' at line 709. Removed '(orchestrator writes inline via jq or Python in a single Bash call — no separate script)' at lines 747-748. Replaced with 'python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID' invocation instruction. Also updated procedural enforcement paragraph to document both role-first and task-first shard patterns.
tasks_completed.rationale: Prior instruction contradicted the no-inline-scripts constraint. Named script is now the canonical path.

tasks_completed.description: Fix A+D — Step 0 parallel auto-detect and Step 1 conditional inspector serialization in close.md
tasks_completed.changes: Inserted '### Step 0 (Parallel-Dev Only): Auto-aggregate missing canonical' BEFORE 'Normal-path artifact preflight'. Three cases: canonical absent + 2+ shards → invoke script + PARALLEL_AGGREGATE_WRITTEN=true; canonical present + 2+ shards → validate via script + PARALLEL_AGGREGATE_WRITTEN=true if action in [aggregated, validated]; ≤1 shard → skip. Step 1 heading updated 'three inspectors in parallel' → 'three inspectors'. Step 1 dispatch conditionalized: parallel cycle detected → sequential dispatch; not parallel → original concurrent dispatch. Summary paragraph updated to remove unconditional 'in parallel'. Invocations use 'python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py'.
tasks_completed.rationale: Parallel-dev cycles with multiple workers caused inspector race conditions when dispatched in parallel. Sequential dispatch prevents concurrent reads of the same aggregated report.

tasks_completed.description: Fix B_contract — Structured status output contract in agents/changelog-analyst.md
tasks_completed.changes: Added '## Structured Final Status Output' section. Defines commit_status ∈ {committed, nothing_to_commit, nothing_to_commit_precommitted, failed}. Three-step SHA-stable nothing_to_commit_precommitted detection: HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse --verify HEAD); git show -s --format=%s for subject check; git show --name-only --format= for file list with blank-line filtering; intersect with task_cycle_files. auto_bulk_commits[] array of {repo_root, branch, sha}. failure_code table: grant_missing/grant_expired/grant_consumed (retryable) vs git_error/staging_error/hook_blocked/scope_violation (non-retryable).
tasks_completed.rationale: Without a structured output contract, commit.md's retry protocol has no reliable way to parse commit results or determine retry eligibility.

tasks_completed.description: Fix P5 — Retry protocol in commands/commit.md; grant revocation in scripts/write-commit-grant.py
tasks_completed.changes: commit.md: Added '### Step 6b: Changelog-analyst result handling and retry protocol'. Handles all 4 status values. Retryable codes invoke python3 ${CONTROL_ROOT}/scripts/write-commit-grant.py --revoke-existing-for-task $TASK_ID then re-dispatch (max 1 retry). Retry applies same status table as initial result (nothing_to_commit_precommitted also treated as success). Non-retryable: print error and stop. write-commit-grant.py: Added --revoke-existing-for-task argparse option. Added _revoke_grants_for_task() scoped to both task_id AND sid (not task_id alone). SID resolved before revocation to prevent deleting grants from other sessions.
tasks_completed.rationale: Grant expiry or consumption during a commit cycle caused unrecoverable failures. Retry protocol allows clean recovery. SID scoping prevents cross-session grant deletion.

scripts_created.purpose: Canonical aggregate writer for parallel-dev cycles. Scans docs/dev/ for per-worker shard dev-reports, validates consistency, writes canonical aggregate.

permissions_to_add.reason: Allow orchestrator to invoke aggregate-dev-report.py for parallel-dev cycles

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — missing features, not a regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Parallel-worker pipeline conflicts: (1) BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE=3 too low; (2) No named script for aggregate construction; (3) No structured commit status contract; (4) No grant retry protocol; (5) Inspector parallelism caused races on parallel-dev canonical.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Raised streak limit; named script replaces inline Python; structured contract enables retry; retry protocol handles grant expiry; conditional serialization prevents inspector races.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: New 432-line script aggregate-dev-report.py is a complete standalone aggregate writer with argparse, validation, union logic, and exit code handling — irreducible below ~380 lines. After codex review, additional validation logic (shard field requirements, canonical comparison) added ~50 lines. The rest is docstrings/comments required for maintainability.

dev.qa_notes: Run the AC checks from acceptance-criteria-dev-20260524-170335.json. Key verifications: (1) grep 'BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 5' hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py returns exactly 1 result; (2) grep 'capped at 3' hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py returns 0; (3) grep 'Bash capped at 3 consecutive' CLAUDE.md returns 0; (4) python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id dev-20260524-170335 --dry-run exits 0 (skipped, 0 shards); (5) agents/changelog-analyst.md contains '## Structured Final Status Output' and 'nothing_to_commit_precommitted'; (6) commands/commit.md contains 'Step 6b' and 'nothing_to_commit_precommitted'; (7) commands/close.md contains 'Step 0 (Parallel-Dev Only)' before 'Normal-path artifact preflight'; (8) python3 -m py_compile scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py exits 0; (9) python3 -m py_compile scripts/write-commit-grant.py exits 0.

blast_radius_declarations.reason: behavioral_test_only:true — file not modified beyond BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE constant and docstring; no behavioral logic changed

tests_run.test: CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$tmp python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id dev-20260524-170335 (2 shards → aggregated)
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$tmp python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id 20260524-000000 (2 shards, canonical present → validated)
malformed shard B.json → exit 1 (Invalid escape in JSON)

recommendations: Consider adding focused tests for aggregate-dev-report.py covering: exact task-id canonical path, suffixed-task isolation, stale canonical mismatch detection (per Codex finding 4 which is now fixed), and --revoke-existing-for-task SID scoping (per Codex finding 11).
Codex finding 8 (DRYRUN not in commit_status enum) and finding 9 (no unambiguous final-line sentinel in changelog-analyst.md) are out-of-scope for this BA spec. File as separate tasks if needed.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning, 90,798 tokens) identified 4 BLOCKERs and 9 MAJOR/REGRESSION_RISK findings. Key issues: (1) canonical_path used bare timestamp instead of full task_id; (2) bare-timestamp shard scanning could mix unrelated suffixed cycles; (3) script not executable (644 permissions); (4) canonical validation was a no-op; (5) validation failure behavior contradicted dev.md contract; (6) missing shard fields (empty string) could pass validation; (7) retry protocol did not treat nothing_to_commit_precommitted as success; (8) hardcoded /root/.claude/scripts/ in commit.md; (9) grant revocation ran before SID validation; (10) stale docs in close.md summary and dev.md Step 9.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 BLOCKERs and 6 of 9 MAJOR items fixed: (1) canonical_path now uses full task_id; (2) shard scanning passes original_task_id through for better isolation; (3) all docs updated to use 'python3 scripts/...' invocation; (4) canonical validation now loads existing canonical and compares parallel_workers + baseline_head_sha; (5) removed unreachable blocked-aggregate logic — fail closed on validation error; (6) _validate_shards now requires non-empty task_id/request_id and baseline_head_sha, and explicit baseline_dirty_snapshot key; (7) retry result now applies same status table as initial (nothing_to_commit_precommitted → continue); (8) hardcoded /root/.claude/scripts/ replaced with ${CONTROL_ROOT}/scripts/; (9) _resolve_sid() called before _revoke_grants_for_task(); (10) revocation scoped to both task_id AND sid; (11) close.md summary paragraph updated; (12) dev.md Step 9 documents both shard patterns. MAJOR items 8 (DRYRUN enum) and 9 (final-line sentinel) deferred as out-of-scope. REGRESSION_RISK 13 (tests) deferred as out-of-scope.

self_verification.notes: python3 -m py_compile scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py → syntax OK. python3 -m py_compile scripts/write-commit-grant.py → syntax OK. python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id dev-20260524-170335 --dry-run → exit 0, action=skipped (0 shards, correct for single-dev cycle). Codex dry-run tests with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$tmp verified aggregation and validation flows. Malformed shard correctly rejected.
